chunks.text: Red Hat Enterprise Linux | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar Red Hat Enterprise Linux The campus has established a site license for Red Hat Enterprise Linux and maintains a local mirror for installation ima

and maintains a local mirror for installation images and patches. Any faculty, staff or student with a valid CalNet ID is eligible to use this service. This service provides a license only, and access to the local mirror of installation images and

ss to the local mirror of installation images and patches. It is assumed the person asking for a license is an experienced system administrator and can perform the installation and ongoing maintenance. If not, a system administration contract can be

e. If not, a system administration contract can be purchased from CUSG which provides this service. To request access to the license key and media, send an email to the IRIS Helpdesk , and we will open a ticket with the campus to request your account

n a ticket with the campus to request your account be added to the EECS Org on the satellite server. Where can I get the latest Red Hat ISOs? The campus FAQ on Red Hat suggests: The easiest way to obtain access to the Red Hat ISOs is to sign up for a

ain access to the Red Hat ISOs is to sign up for a Red Hat account (or use an existing one to login) at https://developers.redhat.com then download the ISOs from https://developers.redhat.com/products/rhel/download See: https://wikihub.berkeley.edu/d

/rhel/download See: https://wikihub.berkeley.edu/display/PIPUB/Red+Hat+Enterprise+Linux+Site+License#RedHatEnterpriseLinuxSiteLicense-FrequentlyAskedQuestions A RedHat Developers account will also give you access to the Red Hat Knowledge Base. Primar

e you access to the Red Hat Knowledge Base. Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
